skill.name: edit-article
skill.description: Edit and improve articles by restructuring sections, improving clarity, and tightening prose. Use when user wants to edit, revise, or improve an article draft.
skill.summary: Edit and improve articles by restructuring sections, improving clarity, and tightening prose. Use wh...
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 for a weak cryptographic algorithm, but this is a false positive. The line is descriptive frontmatter for an article editing workflow and contains no cryptographic code, scripts, network access, or prompt injection text.

content.value_statement: Long drafts can lose readers when sections appear in the wrong order or paragraphs run too long. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured article revision.
actual_capabilities: Divides an article draft into sections based on its headings.
Reviews section order for dependency and flow problems.
Asks the user to confirm the section plan before rewriting.
Rewrites each section for clarity, coherence, and flow.
Keeps rewritten paragraphs within a 240 character limit.
limitations: It does not verify facts or citations in the article.
It depends on the user to provide the article draft.
It does not define a final editing pass after each section rewrite.
It may need user guidance for tone, audience, and publication style.

use_cases.title: Revise Technical Articles
use_cases.description: Improve section order and paragraph clarity before publishing a technical article.

use_cases.title: Tighten Blog Drafts
use_cases.description: Turn a rough blog draft into shorter sections with clearer progression.

use_cases.title: Prepare Editorial Feedback
use_cases.description: Create a structured revision plan before rewriting each part of an article.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner revision of a complete article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the edit-article skill on this draft. First divide it into sections, then ask me to confirm the plan before rewriting.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review focused on ordering ideas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the edit-article skill to review whether the sections appear in the right order. Explain any dependency issues before rewriting.

prompt_templates.title: Shorten Paragraphs
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused pass for readability and paragraph length.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the edit-article skill to rewrite each section for clarity. Keep every paragraph under 240 characters.

prompt_templates.title: Publication Revision
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced editing with audience and style constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the edit-article skill for a publication-ready revision. Preserve my argument, improve structure, and keep the tone direct and professional.

output_examples.input: A draft article with headings, long paragraphs, and several ideas introduced out of order.
output_examples.output: A proposed section map, notes about ordering problems, and a request for confirmation before rewriting.

output_examples.input: A confirmed section plan for a technical blog post.
output_examples.output: Rewritten sections with clearer transitions, tighter sentences, and short paragraphs.

best_practices: Provide the full draft with headings so the section analysis is useful.
Confirm or adjust the section plan before asking for rewritten text.
State the target audience and desired tone before the rewrite begins.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a fact checker for claims, data, or citations.
Do not skip the section confirmation step for complex drafts.
Do not expect it to preserve unclear wording that needs editorial judgment.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant through restructuring and rewriting an article draft for clarity and flow.

faq.question: Does it rewrite the whole article at once?
faq.answer: No. It first divides the article into sections and asks the user to confirm the section plan.

faq.question: Can it improve technical writing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can improve structure, paragraph length, coherence, and sentence clarity in technical articles.

faq.question: Does it verify facts?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on editing and structure, not independent fact checking.

faq.question: Why does it mention a directed acyclic graph?
faq.answer: That instruction helps the assistant order ideas so dependent concepts appear after their prerequisites.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
